channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Iran Flexes Its Cyber Chops
item.description: Hackers linked to the regime are escalating attacks against the United States and Israel as the war rages.

item.title: Xi’s Innovation Paradox
item.description: Recent mandates show the Chinese leader wants both loyalty and creativity from officials.

item.title: Iran Says It Will Target U.S. Tech Companies in the Middle East
item.description: The threat comes as U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned of a decisive next few days in the war.

item.title: “Liberation Year” has not freed American factories
item.description: Even the winners from Donald Trump’s trade war are feeling squeezed

item.title: Five Scenarios for a U.S. Ground War on Iran
item.description: A vexing geography offers no clean entry point.

item.title: The perils of a ground war in Iran
item.description: Donald Trump is deploying troops. It’s not clear he knows what to do with them

item.title: Hurricane Trump threatens to blow China off course
item.description: Chinese elites do not agree on who may win from the war in Iran

item.title: Index providers shouldn’t bend the rules for Elon Musk
item.description: They will only expose ordinary investors to unnecessary risks

item.title: The energy shock brings coal back into fashion
item.description: An LNG crunch is good news for the world’s dirtiest fuel

item.title: Is China covering up a violent attack at a market in Beijing?
item.description: News outlets put a premium on making the country seem harmonious

item.title: Is China covering up a violent attack at a Beijing market?
item.description: News outlets put a premium on making the country seem harmonious

item.title: Cuba’s Third Chance
item.description: While the ongoing US oil blockade has exacerbated Cuba’s economic and humanitarian crisis, the primary responsibility for its current predicament lies with the communist regime. If Cubans use this crisis as an opportunity to pursue ambitious economic and political reforms, they may yet restore the prosperity they once knew.

item.title: Does Iran’s Future Look Like Cuba, Syria, or North Korea?
item.description: The Gulf and Israel are pursuing rival strategies toward Iran, but neither is likely to get what they want.

item.title: The Islamic State Sahel Threat Is Transnational
item.description: Jihadism’s global hub is shifting, and its reach expanding.

item.title: International Law Is Still Worth Defending
item.description: While a world where power trumps principle is hostile to small states, it is also bad for great powers. The challenges that will shape our future do not respect borders or recognize spheres of influence, and they can be contained only through binding agreements and institutionalized cooperation.

item.title: Those Who Cannot Remember the Past Condemn Others to Repeat It
item.description: History has always been misused, but Washington’s Iran rhetoric is uniquely egregious.

item.title: Fuel Shortages Raise Stability Risks in Myanmar
item.description: After a string of political victories, the military regime might now grapple with fresh upheaval.

item.title: What the State Department Got Wrong With the Iran War Evacuations
item.description: Ex-diplomats point to a lack of qualified senior staff and a breakdown in trust.

item.title: Where Do Kazakhs Get Their News?
item.description: A recent survey dives into the media consumption habits of Kazakhs.

item.title: Japan and South Korea’s Energy Hedge
item.description: For Japan and South Korea, strategic bilateral cooperation has become essential to individual energy security.

item.title: China’s Economy Feels the Iran War Shock
item.description: And it was already struggling.

item.title: How Donald Trump Doubled Down on the Rhetorical Presidency in His Handling of China
item.description: “The Highly Respected President of China”? To better anticipate Trump’s China policy, it is essential to understand his rhetorical strategy.

item.title: Iran-Israel-US War’s Quiet Shock on India’s Fertilizer Imports
item.description: India must redesign its structures of dependence on external sources of fertilizer in the face of the ongoing Gulf war.

item.title: Breaking Down the Belarus-North Korea Summit
item.description: Lukashenko’s visit reflects a broader evolution in North Korea’s foreign policy, which is slowly expanding in the post-COVID era and is increasingly influenced by ties with the Kremlin.

item.title: A Complacent America Shrugs Off New War Technologies
item.description: From the president on down, many Americans still do not grasp the implications of drones and other threats.

item.title: Building the Middle-Power World Order
item.description: If Canada and other "middle powers" are serious about building a different kind of global order based on "institutions and agreements that function as described," the time to start planning is now. Only with a clear vision can they hope to make a difference when the next big crisis forces a search for alternatives.

item.title: Russia’s Sanctions-Busting Cryptocurrency Empire
item.description: A Kremlin-backed fintech company is linked to massive trade in dual-use goods.

item.title: Nuclear Deterrence Is No Longer Enough
item.description: Today, violent conflicts are increasingly interconnected, with each new hotspot amplifying others and increasing the strain on the system as a whole. Under such conditions, wars no longer remain separate but can converge into a single strategic crisis – all while remaining below the nuclear threshold.

item.title: What the Supreme Court will make of birthright citizenship
item.description: Donald Trump’s fringe view of the 14th Amendment is about to get its day in court

item.title: Independent Journalism Finds a Way
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has sought to tame Hungary’s independent media outlets through regulatory engineering, financial pressure, and ownership concentration. But he never fully defeated them, and their reporting on the regime’s corruption and abuses of power has helped fuel the opposition’s rise ahead of April’s election.

item.title: Indian Army is Short of Artillery, the Modern Battlefield’s Most Lethal Killer
item.description: It has about 226 field artillery regiments today and plans to increase that to 270 regiments.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s Energy Emergency Begins
item.description: The Philippines has seen one of the world’s sharpest increase in petrol prices.

item.title: Iraq’s Uniquely Fraught Position in the Iran War
item.description: Its northern Kurdistan Region is being hit by both sides.

item.title: How Geopolitics Overran Globalization
item.description: The end of the dream of economic integration.

item.title: The Iran War Comes to Iraq
item.description: Baghdad slides into a conflict it long wanted to escape.

item.title: The Real War for Iran’s Future
item.description: Who will determine the fate of the Islamic Republic?

item.title: In the Philippines, Technology-facilitated Violence Is Depriving Girls of an Education
item.description: Access to education means little if girls are denied protection from harassment, cyber-bullying, and online sexual exploitation.

item.title: The Iran War Is Threatening Another Economic Crisis in Laos
item.description: The country of 8 million is still dealing with the impacts of a post-COVID crisis that was marked by high inflation and a collapse in the value of the kip.

item.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Resigns as Head of Armed Forces, Prepares to Take Presidency
item.description: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing has engineered a civilian "transition" that will cement the military's control over Myanmar's political and economic life.

item.title: The Rise of Chinese Organized Crime in Chile
item.description: China-linked criminal networks employ legal businesses to conceal illicit activities ranging from human trafficking to scam and drug operations.

item.title: Calls for Deeper Investigations Grow in Kyrgyzstan
item.description: A former Kyrgyz prosecutor general has called on the Kyrgyz government to create an independent commission to review the activities of the State Committee for National Security (SCNS) since 2020.

item.title: Are India-US Ties Really Back on Track?
item.description: Despite high-level meetings and a Modi-Trump phone call in March, the ground reality reveals that a reset in relations remains elusive.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Disrupting Balochistan’s Economy
item.description: The world is preoccupied with energy prices and economic shocks. Meanwhile, the plight of Balochistan, which shares a long border with Iran, is being ignored.
